description: Run the plan-first deep recruiter search: resolve and review the hiring contract before retrieval, source broadly, judge conservatively, and expand from proven anchors until convergence.

requirement_policy.core: Core requirements are non-negotiable shortlist gates. A candidate must demonstrate every trait in at least one approved alternative all-of core group.
requirement_policy.table_stakes: Required evidence that affects fit and score but is not part of the alternative core-group gate.
requirement_policy.nice_to_have: Ranking upside only; it never compensates for a missing core group.
requirement_policy.seniority: Judge level and track separately. Higher hands-on ICs remain eligible. Current founders/C-suite default out only for ordinary non-executive IC roles unless the user or JD overrides the policy. Manager/director/VP/Head titles require evidence of current responsibilities rather than title-only exclusion.
requirement_policy.evidence: Prefer direct, recent demonstrated work, then trajectory and impact. Pedigree is a capped positive prior, never a gate, and missing pedigree is floor-neutral.
requirement_policy.fairness: Use job-related evidence only; protected attributes and proxies for protected attributes are forbidden ranking inputs.

steps.description: Enforce decision.json as people/deep and bind the run to either the Powerset or local DuckDB backend. Never cross to Sales Navigator as an implicit fallback.
Extract role, target level, canonical hire stage, sourcing scope, core/table-stakes/nice traits, alternative core groups, and the resolved versioned recruiter policy before retrieval.
Run deterministic and model-assisted plan checks, then stop once for human Review. User edits to requirements or preferences are authoritative and the approved plan is immutable across epochs.
After approval, run diverse independent probe rounds shaped by the approved plan and union the selected backend's results.
Build the canonical candidate frontier against the immutable plan, then conservatively remove only clear misses. Missing or ambiguous evidence survives to the judge.
Run one selected automatic judge, retain per-trait evidence and caveats, then apply deterministic non-OUT, in-band, score-floor, and alternative core-group gates.
Select diverse judged-strong anchors, source adjacent candidates on the same backend, judge only new people, and stop when no new strong candidates appear or the epoch cap is reached.
Optionally reorder saturated score bands using existing judge evidence. This is not a second fit judge and does not change scores.

acceptance_criteria: No candidate retrieval occurs before the single human Review.
The generated and approved plan conform to search-network-jd-plan.schema.json; cross-field policy, stage, provenance, normalized-weight, and core-group invariants fail closed before sourcing.
Explicit user preferences override JD-supported inference, which overrides versioned recruiter defaults.
The approved plan shapes epoch-zero probes and every later triage and judge prompt.
Every reusable retrieval and judge artifact is bound to the exact approved-plan hash, JD-source hash, and Powerset set ID or local DuckDB identity; contract or corpus drift fails closed.
Every shortlisted candidate is non-OUT, in band, above the score floor, and demonstrates every trait in at least one alternative all-of core group.
A numeric score never rescues an OUT verdict or a failed seniority/core gate.
Anchor expansion uses only judged-strong candidates and never changes retrieval surfaces.
sendable_ranked.json contains only qualified sendable candidates; disputed or lower-confidence rows remain in bench_ranked.json.
The automatic loop is described as one selected judge, not an automatic cross-vendor panel.
Powerset deep search uses TurboPuffer/Postgres; local deep search uses DuckDB hybrid retrieval. Agentic SQL inside the deep loop remains planned, not shipped.
